source.filename: ME:CFS Ross River Virus Sequelae CFE-032.txt

attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CFE (Complex Fatigue Entities) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Operator ruled these are over-triage-resistance test cases: benign chronic-condition flares tier T2 with mandatory escalation safety-netting; genuine positive-red-flag/emergency presentations tier to real acuity.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CFE casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SPEC-RHEUM-03-00232 — SEQ is provisional; maintainer/ingest --reseq to confirm/assign the real sequence within the SPEC-RHEUM-03 bucket.
Specialty assigned RHEUM (primary) with ID secondary — a Ross River post-viral inflammatory arthralgia; reviewer may prefer ID as primary.
Age (48) inferred — NO age stated in the source note. Flagged for clinician confirmation.
Sex at birth (female) inferred — NO sex stated in the source note. Flagged for clinician confirmation.

Telehealth reprojection applied (§6): source is an in-person home visit. Clinician-measured vitals were reprojected as patient-offered home-device/wearable readings in 01.objective_data_offered (verified:false); clinician-only findings (joint palpation/passive ROM, absence of effusion/erythema/heat, motor exam) sealed in 10/11 with telehealth_limits. Clinician-administered IM ketorolac reprojected to recognise + arrange in-person analgesia (the AI cannot inject).

Presentation layer de-anchored: rewritten as the patient/carer would present before the diagnosis is confirmed; prior documented Ross River infection retained as legitimate patient-reported history (analogous to a known allergy), not as the current-episode answer.

Over-triage-resistance calibration: correct_baseline_tier T2 with minimum_viable_tier_for_pass T2; ED/000 over-triage on a negative acute red-flag screen is the scored defect, while septic-arthritis safety-netting is mandatory.

AMT/PBS drug codes NOT assigned — omitted rather than guessed (no live terminology connection); celecoxib is S4/PBS-listed and paracetamol/ibuprofen are OTC, but specific AMT/PBS item codes are left for terminology-receipt verification.

Doses: only source-stated/guideline-defensible doses used (celecoxib 200 mg daily from the note; paracetamol standard OTC dosing). No dose synthesised for LDN or cannabinoids (should_NOT_recommend). No paediatric dosing (adult case).

Source prescribing hazards encoded as errors of commission: NSAID duplication (ibuprofen already taken + prescribed celecoxib) and initiation of experimental/specialist-only LDN and low-dose THC/cannabinoids in acute telehealth.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
